description.value:
A Hazard or Item that is constructed or developed to hinder creatures who encounter it.

Traps can only be made with a Toolkit listed in the following chart.

Additionally, all Traps have Rank based variants that increases its difficulty and risk associated with it.

All traps requires proficiency in both the Toolkit required and the Crafting skill to even attempt to make a trap.

All traps requires an Intelligence (Toolkit) or Intelligence (Crafting) check, while maintaining access to the listed Toolkit for the duration to create.

Attempting to create a trap costs 1 charge of a Trappers Kit or its listed Toolkit.

A Trappers kit can be used to create all listed Traps, but unless specified cannot be used to disable all listed traps.

A Security kit can be used to Disable all listed traps, but cannot be used to create a trap.

All Traps begin at D-Rank and increases in stature by 1 degree every rank up until S-Rank.

For every Rank a builder would attempt to increase a Traps Rank by, they increase the DC required to build the trap (Build DC) by +4 and the Save and Notice/Disable DC of the trap by +2.

If a creature beats the Build DC by 5 or more, the Traps Save DC is increased by +1 and the Notice/Disable DC is increased by +2, for every 5 the creature beat the Build DC by.

Additionally for every Rank, beyond D-Rank a trap is, it's damage, if any is increased by +2 damage die.

These traps requires an active Wisdom (Perception) or Intelligence (Investigation) check vs its Notice DC to be noticed.

If a trap is noticed prior to it being triggered, then a creature with the appropriate Toolkit can attempt to disable it so long as they are within 5 feet of the triggering area.

On a failed check, the trap triggers anyway.

Finally, the constructor of a Trap can make a trap either a Snare or Trigger based trap.

A snare-based trap is a trap that has a set trigger and cannot be activated manually except by an unsuccessful Disable attempt.

A trigger- based trap is a trap that can only be manually or remotely activated by its maker or whomever they designate as a triggering creature.

Alarming Trap.

You create an alarm snare by rigging one or more noisy objects to a trip wire, pressure plate or manual trigger.

When you create an alarm snare, you designate a range between 100 to 500 feet at which it can be heard.

When a Small or larger creature enters the square, the snare makes a noise loud enough that it can be heard by all creatures in the range you designated.

Deadfall Trap.

A weight-sensitive plate buried underneath sand or a sensitive wire holding a boulder, large tree or some other Large object over a cliff ledge, causing it to fall on targets within 45 feet of the target space.

Select a 15-foot cube shaped space as the trigger space, if this trap is set to a trigger.

Next select a 15-foot wide, 60-foot line that this trap affects.

All ranges and spaces must be selected prior to the activation of this trap.

All creatures in the lines range must make a Saving throw, taking 2d10 + (5 times Rank) bludgeoning damage, or half as much on a success.

Drowning Pit Trap.

A trapdoor covers a 10-foot- square pit that's 30 feet deep and has 5 feet of water at the bottom.

Four water spouts in the walls connect to hidden water tanks.

Each water spout extends out of a different wall, 6 inches from the top of the pit.

A creature who walks onto the trap door triggers this trap.

The triggering creature falls into the pit and takes falling damage.

A creature can attempt to make their saving throw to avoid falling in on a success.

Regardless the trapdoor then immediately closes shut, and this trap rolls Initiative using a +10 bonus.

As an action and Bonus action the trap fills the pit with 5 feet of water for each action spent.

This trap loses its bonus action when 2 of the water spouts are disabled, and its Action when all 4 of the water spouts are disabled.

If the pit would fill with water up to the top (30 feet), the trap stops filling with water, and the creature inside begins drowning.

This trap can be reset by reengaging the door and refilling the water tanks.

Explosive Trap.

You create an explosive trap that utilizes multiple Paper bombs of the same type that all explodes with a creature enters the traps square, or when the trap is manually triggered.

This trap is set on a 5-foot space.

All creatures within 10 feet of this trap when it is triggered makes its saving throw taking 8d4 fire damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

A creature who fails their saving throw by 5 or more, takes double damage.

Flashing Trap.

You create an explosive trap that utilizes multiple Flash Tags of the same type that all explodes with a creature enters the traps square, or when the trap is manually triggered.

This trap is set on a 5-foot space.

All creatures within 10 feet of this trap when it is triggered makes its saving throw being blinded for 2d4 rounds on a failed save or half as much on a success.

A creature who fails their saving throw by 5 or more, is blinded and dazed for 1 minute.

Hidden Pit Trap.

A trapdoor covers a 10-foot-square pit that's 30 feet deep and has 5 feet of water at the bottom.

A creature who walks onto the trap door triggers this trap.

The triggering creature falls into the pit and takes falling damage.

A creature can attempt to make their saving throw to avoid falling in on a success.

This trap can be reset by reengaging the door.

Poisonous Lock.

You create a sprint loaded lock with a poisoned spine near the keyhole.

Breaking this lock does not disengage the poison spine.

A creature that attempts to unlock or pick this lock without the correct key triggers this trap.

When this trap is triggered, the triggering creature is stabbed with the poisoned spine forcing them to make a saving throw.

On a failed save they are poisoned and envenomed for 1 hour.

A creature who fails their saving throw by 5 or more, instead gains 3 ranks of envenomed.

Poisonous Trap.

You create an explosive trap that utilizes multiple Poison Tags of the same type that all explodes with a creature enters the traps square, or when the trap is manually triggered.

This trap is set on a 5-foot space.

All creatures within 10 feet of this trap when it is triggered makes its saving throw being poisoned for 1d4 minutes on a failed save or half as much on a success.

A creature who fails their saving throw by 5 or more, is Poisoned and Envenomed 2d4 minutes.

Restraining Trap.

You create a trap made to capture creatures who enter the traps space.

This trap is set on a 5-foot space.

All creatures within 10 feet of this trap when it is triggered makes its saving throw being grappled and restrained on a failed save.

A creature who fails their saving throw by 5 or more, is stunned and paralyzed until the end of their next turn, and afterwards grappled and restrained for the next minute.

Creatures restrained or grappled in this way, remakes this save at the end of each of their turns ending effects on a pass.

Shocking Trap.

You create an explosive trap that utilizes multiple Shock Bombs of the same type that all explodes with a creature enters the traps space , or when the trap is manually triggered.

This trap is set on a 5-foot space.

All creatures within 10 feet of this trap when it is triggered makes its saving throw taking 8d6 lighting damage on a failed save or half as much on a success.

A creature who fails their saving throw by 5 or more, takes double damage.

Weapon Trap.

You create a trap that utilizes multiple small weapons (Kunai, Shuriken, Senbon etc.) that all fire overwhelming the target space when a creature enters the traps square, or when the trap is manually triggered.

This trap is set on a 5-foot space.

All creatures within 15 feet of this trap when it is triggered makes its saving throw taking 9d4 Slashing or Piercing damage (Pick one) on a failed save or half as much on a success.

A creature who fails their saving throw by 5 or more, takes double damage and gains 2 ranks of bleeding.

Yellow Mold.

You cultivate a poisonous spore of mold that assaults nearby creatures.

This trap is triggered when a creature moves into the molds space, damages, or fails to disable the mold.

When the trap is triggered, all creatures within 10 feet are exposed to the mold.

They must make a Saving throw every turn for the next minute.

Each failed save inflicts them with 1 rank of the envenomed condition, which persists for 24 Hours.
